_meta.task: wood-33 (M1) — centralize coaching copy keyed by block/exercise (extraction slice)
_meta.keyScheme: day.<weekday>.<module>.<preset>.why for the daily-plan backbone; <section>.<exercise/tool>.<role> for static section coaching. Keys are stable & hierarchical, derived from each block's module+preset fields.

deferred.reason: runtime value, not static copy; it CONSUMES copy rather than defining it

deferred.what: Interpolated class="sub" strings containing ${...} (e.g. ~L2039 interval side-tool, ~L2882 Saturday/pinned-tune line, ~L3319 session-log empty state)
deferred.reason: templated strings that splice variables; extract during wiring with a tokenized format, not as flat text
borderline UI microcopy; include in a later pass once the COPY() loader exists
out of scope for this slice (coaching copy first); catalogue later if desired

day.monday.gym.warmup.why.text: Cichowicz first: wind before fingers. Tone is the product; everything else is packaging.
day.monday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.monday.gym.ladder.why.text: Five choruses, quarters to 16th-note triplets, over a band that won't lie to you. This is how a subdivision ceiling gets raised.
day.monday.gym.ladder.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / gym.ladder block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.monday.vocab.lickday.why.text: Vocabulary enters through the ear. Hear it, find it, THEN read it. Three keys minimum.
day.monday.vocab.lickday.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / vocab.lickday block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.monday.tunes.tuneweek.why.text: Melody from the embedded page, roots from memory with the band. Foundation pass.
day.monday.tunes.tuneweek.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / tunes.tuneweek block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.monday.ears.transcribe.why.text: Five minutes inside the week's solo. Loop one phrase at 0.5×, sing it cold, find it on the horn. This is the rep that actually builds the ear.
day.monday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.monday.today.lore.why.text: One card, one listening assignment, three sentences about what felt hard.
day.monday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Monday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.gym.warmup.why.text: Slurs are the trumpet's legato gym — full arc plus flexibility 8ths today.
day.tuesday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.gym.clarke.why.text: Clarke II at PR-10, PR-5, PR, PR+2. Log it. Speed is built 2 bpm at a time, sotto voce.
day.tuesday.gym.clarke.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / gym.clarke block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.vocab.cycle.why.text: Circle of fourths with the band behind you. A lick in 12 keys is language, not a trick.
day.tuesday.vocab.cycle.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / vocab.cycle block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.tunes.guides.why.text: 3rds & 7ths are the tune's skeleton — solos that "make the changes" are built on these two notes.
day.tuesday.tunes.guides.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / tunes.guides block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.ears.transcribe.why.text: Steal one phrase from the week's solo, then sing it back against the tuner. Voice first, valves second — if you can't sing it, you don't own it.
day.tuesday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.tuesday.today.lore.why.text: Read. Queue the commute listening. One sentence.
day.tuesday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Tuesday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.gym.warmup.why.text: Same ritual, deeper breath. Watch the tuner needle on your long tones today.
day.wednesday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.gym.bootcamp.why.text: 16th bursts, sextuplet pyramid, double-tongue leaps — double-time fluency, built directly.
day.wednesday.gym.bootcamp.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / gym.bootcamp block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.vocab.review.why.text: Three days ago's lick, four keys, from memory. Spaced repetition beats cramming.
day.wednesday.vocab.review.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / vocab.review block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.tunes.memorize.why.text: Sing → melody from memory → roots → guide tones → solo eyes-closed. Climb one rung.
day.wednesday.tunes.memorize.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / tunes.memorize block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.ears.transcribe.why.text: Five minutes inside the week's assigned solo. Even one phrase sung accurately counts.
day.wednesday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.wednesday.today.lore.why.text: One card. One listen. One sentence.
day.wednesday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Wednesday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.gym.warmup.why.text: Slur sets expand a semitone past comfort today. Gently.
day.thursday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.gym.clarke.why.text: Moving-line single-tongue test, then double-tongue leaps, then Clarke III. New PR or no dessert.
day.thursday.gym.clarke.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / gym.clarke block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.vocab.sprint.why.text: Today's lick at target bpm in its 4 hardest keys. Sloppy = drop 10; clean ×3 = up 5.
day.thursday.vocab.sprint.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / vocab.sprint block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.tunes.solo.why.text: Three choruses, no chart if possible. Record one. You're building the Saturday muscle.
day.thursday.tunes.solo.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / tunes.solo block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.ears.transcribe.why.text: One phrase from the week's solo, by ear. Descending lines are where real transcription gets hard — chase those today.
day.thursday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.thursday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Thursday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.gym.warmup.why.text: Friday rule: warm up like it's a gig night, because someday it will be.
day.friday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.gym.pyramid.why.text: Quarters to sextuplets on one note, one breath line. Time-feel maintenance.
day.friday.gym.pyramid.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / gym.pyramid block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.vocab.cycle.why.text: Drop the lick into a moving ii-V cycle — vocabulary only counts when it survives traffic.
day.friday.vocab.cycle.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / vocab.cycle block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.tunes.solo.why.text: Book closed. Band on. Train wheels-off on Friday and Saturday's jam-sim feels like a rerun.
day.friday.tunes.solo.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / tunes.solo block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.ears.transcribe.why.text: End the work week by stealing one more phrase from the week's solo. Five minutes, one phrase you can sing back clean.
day.friday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.friday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Friday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.saturday.gym.warmup.why.text: Short and honest — the pre-jam routine you'll use in the wild.
day.saturday.gym.warmup.why.context: CURRICULUM / Saturday / gym.warmup block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.saturday.tunes.jamset.why.text: Tune of the week + 2 review tunes. Head in, choruses, head out. No restarts — recovering live IS the skill.
day.saturday.tunes.jamset.why.context: CURRICULUM / Saturday / tunes.jamset block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.saturday.ears.transcribe.why.text: While the adrenaline's up: grab one phrase from the week's solo by ear.
day.saturday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Saturday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.saturday.today.lore.why.text: What broke down? That's Monday's assignment. Write it while it stings.
day.saturday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Saturday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.sunday.gym.drone.why.text: Light day — chops rebuild on rest. Long tones INTO the drone until beats disappear.
day.sunday.gym.drone.why.context: CURRICULUM / Sunday / gym.drone block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.sunday.ears.intervals.why.text: Voice first, horn second. Singing the week's licks files them in long-term storage.
day.sunday.ears.intervals.why.context: CURRICULUM / Sunday / ears.intervals block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.sunday.ears.transcribe.why.text: The week's big ear session: 2-4 bars you OWN beat twenty you visited. Half speed, full attention.
day.sunday.ears.transcribe.why.context: CURRICULUM / Sunday / ears.transcribe block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.sunday.tunes.tuneweek.why.text: Last week's tune, melody and one easy chorus. Keep the shelf stocked.
day.sunday.tunes.tuneweek.why.context: CURRICULUM / Sunday / tunes.tuneweek block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

day.sunday.today.lore.why.text: Glance at Progress. Pick one number to move by Friday.
day.sunday.today.lore.why.context: CURRICULUM / Sunday / today.lore block — one-line 'why' coaching rationale

gym.cichowicz.range.coach.text: Crescendo INTO the top note; don't grab it. Three arcs max, full rest between, stop on the first thin one.
gym.cichowicz.range.coach.context: Full Workout / Cichowicz flow / range-arc card

gym.cichowicz.rest.coach.text: Rest = play. Bell on knee between reps. Tone first, range second, ego never.
gym.cichowicz.rest.coach.context: Full Workout / Cichowicz flow / rest reminder

gym.ladder.coach.text: Rhythm is the instrument; notes are incidental. If a chorus feels easy you're not subdividing honestly. Survive chorus 5 clean and log it.

gym.clarke.coach.text: Play straight from the book — no on-screen approximation. Fingers ride the air column; the rep that smears doesn't count — clean only, then +2 bpm.
gym.clarke.coach.context: Full Workout / Clarke card — play from the book

gym.bootcamp.fromBook.coach.text: Run it from the book — no on-screen approximation. Start at the bottom of the metronome ramp.
gym.bootcamp.fromBook.coach.context: Full Workout / Double-Time Bootcamp — run from the book

gym.bootcamp.math.coach.text: Remember the math: 16th triplets at 100 = 8th triplets at 200. Slow is fast.
gym.bootcamp.math.coach.context: Full Workout / Double-Time Bootcamp — subdivision math

tunes.jamset.noStop.coach.text: No stopping, no restarts. Get lost? Guide tones + simple rhythm until the form comes back — that IS the skill. Debrief in the journal after.
tunes.jamset.noStop.coach.context: Tunes / THE SET (jam-set) — no stopping rule

ears.intervals.sing.coach.text: Tuner on (toolbar). Sing today's lick and last review lick — needle honest, no horn. If you can't sing it, you're typing.
ears.intervals.sing.coach.context: Ears / interval trainer — sing, tuner honest

ears.transcribe.steal.coach.text: The 5-minute steal: ONE phrase. (1) Loop it til you can sing it cold. (2) Find it on the horn — no paper. (3) Play WITH the record til your articulation matches. Player ⚙ → 0.5×. Log it when you land it.

gym.dropTime.coach.text: Per-block notes now live inside each block. Auto-save to your folder arrives with the morning-rebuild loop.

vocab.lickday.howto.coach.text: Ear first. Then notation. Then all 12 keys. Then over a moving band. That's how a lick becomes YOURS.
vocab.lickday.howto.coach.context: Vocab / Lick of the Day — ear→notation→keys→band

gym.home.coach.text: Your gym blocks live here — practice any of them freely, no timer needed. The timed Session is just a guided walk through these same blocks. The toolkit below stays open.

gym.tool.metronomeRamp.coach.text: Auto-raises the metronome every N bars. Set it and chase it.

gym.tool.clickDropout.coach.text: Click vanishes for N bars, then returns to judge you. Internal clock court.

gym.tool.doubleTime.coach.text: Moving-scale 16ths (fingers and tongue must sync). Raise until articulation smears, back off 4 — that's today's number. Then the double-tongue leap cell below. Most players' double-time ceiling is the tongue.

gym.tool.clarkePR.coach.text: PR protocol: PR−10 once, PR−5 once, PR once, PR+2 attempt. Log only CLEAN.

ears.home.coach.text: This is your ears home — today's ear block leads, both trainers stay open, then the canon, the lore deck, and the Day Review. The timed Session walks you through these same tools.

ears.canon.coach.text: One column of history, one thing to steal from each. Work through these on commutes. 2:1 rule: listen twice as much as you shed.

vocab.matrix.coach.text: Columns = circle of fourths from C. Fill a row and that lick is LANGUAGE.

lore.principle1.timeBeatsNotes.text: The band forgives a wrong note in 50 milliseconds and never forgives rushing. Most double-time problems are TIME-FEEL problems, not finger-speed problems — hence the daily Subdivision Ladder and the 2&4 click. When the pocket is yours, doubled-up lines are just subdivision you already own, with pitches attached.
lore.principle1.timeBeatsNotes.context: Lore deck / principle 1 — Time beats notes

lore.principle2.vocabNotTheory.text: Clark Terry: imitate → assimilate → innovate. Nobody speaks by spelling grammar rules; they steal phrases and wear them until they fit. 5 licks × 12 keys, ear-first, over a moving band = a phrasebook that becomes reflex. The theory is the receipt, not the engine.
lore.principle2.vocabNotTheory.context: Lore deck / principle 2 — Vocabulary, not theory

lore.principle3.tunesCurrency.text: Jam sessions don't ask what you know — they call tunes. A memorized tune frees 100% of your attention for LISTENING, which is where the music actually happens. Five rungs per tune, one tune a week. In 12 weeks you own the dozen that gets called everywhere on Earth.
lore.principle3.tunesCurrency.context: Lore deck / principle 3 — Tunes are the currency

lore.principle4.soundFirst.text: Your tone is your handshake. Ten minutes of flow studies and drone long-tones daily compounds into the sound people turn around for. Wind before fingers, always.

lore.principle5.softBuildsFast.text: Clarke's studies — from YOUR book, deep-linked here — played sotto voce build the efficiency that makes speed cheap. PR protocol: −10, −5, PR, +2. Two bpm a day is 60 a month.
lore.principle5.softBuildsFast.context: Lore deck / principle 5 — Soft builds fast (Clarke)

lore.principle6.restIsRep.text: The embouchure is the only part of jazz that punishes overtime. Rest as much as you play inside every block. Sunday is deliberately light. Tired chops learn lies.
lore.principle6.restIsRep.context: Lore deck / principle 6 — Rest is a rep

lore.arc12week.coach.text: Weeks 1–4: subdivision ceiling to 16ths-at-120, three tunes owned, six licks in 12 keys. Weeks 5–8: double-time bursts in real solos, six tunes, twelve licks. Weeks 9–12: full dozen, Saturday jam-sets feel easy, and any session is a room you walk into like you've been there — because in here, you have. Every day this portal already knows what to do. Trust the rotation; spend your willpower on the horn.

lore.jam.before.coach.text: Warm up BEFORE you arrive (the Saturday 8-min routine). Bring the horn warm. Know 3 tunes cold at 3 tempos — better than 20 tunes at 70%. Scout the session once before playing it.

lore.jam.callingTune.coach.text: Call YOUR tune (this week's), in the standard key, at a tempo you own. Count it off with confidence: snap on 2 & 4, "one… two… one-two-three-four." A confident count-in buys you the band's respect before you play a note.
lore.jam.callingTune.coach.context: Lore deck / jam craft — Calling a tune

lore.jam.onStand.coach.text: Melody is yours if you called it. 2–3 choruses max — leave them wanting. Can't hear the form? Play guide tones and SIMPLE rhythm until it locks; nobody hears "lost," they hear "patient." End phrases ON chord tones, beats 1 or 3.
lore.jam.onStand.coach.context: Lore deck / jam craft — On the stand

lore.jam.etiquette.coach.text: Listen twice as much as you play. Acknowledge solos (a nod beats a "woo"). Don't noodle between tunes. Thank the house band and the host BY NAME. Stay for the set after yours.
lore.jam.etiquette.coach.context: Lore deck / jam craft — Etiquette physics

lore.jam.sideways.coach.text: It will — that's tuition, not a verdict. Note WHAT specifically broke (tempo? form? vocabulary? nerves?) in the journal that night. It becomes Monday's block. The players who sound untouchable are a stack of practice weeks ahead, nothing more.
lore.jam.sideways.coach.context: Lore deck / jam craft — When a set goes sideways

lore.jam.quietWeapon.coach.text: Almost nobody at a jam plays with great TIME and great SOUND. Solos that are 80% rhythm with a beautiful tone beat note-storms every single week. You're a strategist — exploit the inefficiency.
lore.jam.quietWeapon.coach.context: Lore deck / jam craft — The quiet weapon
